events.description: Act of Congress removed restrictions on mixed blood Indians of the Five Civilized Tribes
Oklahoma admitted into the Union as a state
Competency commission authorized for work in district comprising Muskogee, McIntosh, and Haskell Counties
Approximately 200 Indians granted unconditional removal of restrictions by competency commission
Approximately 125 half-blood Indians granted unconditional removal of restrictions by blanket order of Secretary of the Interior
Mollie Johnson executed deeds conveying 160-acre allotment to various parties without fair consideration, loss $750
Daniel Johnson prevailed upon to deed away land for practically nothing, loss $900
Caney Arbor executed various deeds at instigation of grafters, loss $900, died destitute
Henderson Arbor defrauded of land, now in destitute circumstances, loss $750

events.name: fraud
events.description: Lizzie Sewell induced to part with land for $800 less than adequate consideration
Tilda Sunday defrauded of land, loss $900
Jemima Harjo induced to sign deed receiving $400 when land worth $1,400, loss $1,000
Jackson Wolf and Milla Wolf declared competent and turned loose by commission despite inability to speak, read, or write English; grafters soon obtained their land
Abijah Colbert and Richard Floyd Colbert turned loose by commission, subsequently disposed of allotments and purchased garage with $20,000 mortgage
All citizens of Cherokee Nation become unrestricted by Act of Congress

financial_transactions.description: Sale of 160-acre allotment (Roll No. 9130) for unfair consideration, appraised value $1,040
Sale of allotment (Roll No. 9131) for practically nothing
Sale of allotment (Roll No. 9613) at instigation of grafters
Sale of allotment (Roll No. 7529) for inadequate consideration
Sale of allotment for $400 when land worth $1,400, loss $1,000

financial_transactions.payer: Abijah Colbert and Richard Floyd Colbert
financial_transactions.description: Purchase of garage assuming $20,000 mortgage after disposing of allotments

correspondence.subject: Request for data concerning unconditional removal of restrictions
correspondence.action_requested: Provide data and opinions on removal of restrictions
correspondence.outcome: Shannon provided detailed report on 60 regular channel removals, 105 competency commission cases, and 100 Departmental order cases

correspondence.subject: Request for data concerning unconditional removal of restrictions
correspondence.outcome: Keith provided report on conditions in Muskogee district

correspondence.subject: Request for data concerning unconditional removal of restrictions
correspondence.action_requested: Provide information on removal of restrictions
correspondence.outcome: Polk provided report on 200 Indians granted removal by commission and 125 by blanket order since Fall 1918

correspondence.subject: Request for information on handling Indian problem
correspondence.action_requested: Provide memorandum on different ways of handling Indian problem
correspondence.outcome: Clements provided recommendations on surplus land sales and home improvements

correspondence.subject: Inquiry on removal of restrictions
correspondence.action_requested: Reply to inquiry of April 3 regarding removal of restrictions
correspondence.outcome: Brauninger provided retrospective analysis and recommendation for individual case handling

correspondence.subject: Present condition among restricted Indians
correspondence.action_requested: Provide opinion on ability of Indians to operate own affairs
correspondence.outcome: McLaughlin reported 80% wasted property and recommended against wholesale removal

legislative_actions.outcome: Removed restrictions on mixed blood Indians of the Five Civilized Tribes
Provided that all citizens of Cherokee Nation become unrestricted in 1931
